methodsAttempted.query: site:sharjah.ac.ae University of Sharjah artificial intelligence policy generative AI ChatGPT academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search surfaced an official University of Sharjah PDF titled Policy on the Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence in Academic Texts, plus lower-specificity official annual-report, conference, and CV PDFs.
A second search surfaced the official ILHE annual report and an academic unit page with an event-style AI title, but no additional direct policy source.
The relevant policy PDF is hosted under the official University of Sharjah domain and robots.txt allows crawling.

methodsAttempted.query: Policy on the Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence in Academic Texts
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl PDF extraction and direct HTTP metadata checks confirmed the PDF was publicly accessible with HTTP 200.

artifacts.summary: Official discovery found one direct University of Sharjah AI policy PDF for graduate theses, graduate-level courses, and academic texts submitted to Graduate Studies bodies. Other official results were rejected for extraction because they were annual-report, research, CV, conference, or event-style sources rather than policy or guidance pages.

stopConditions: Stop after the official Graduate Studies AI academic texts policy PDF is fetched and evidence snippets are extracted.
Do not use annual reports, CVs, conference booklets, event listings, or research showcases as policy claims.
Do not mark any staged claim as human reviewed or canonical.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy on the Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence in Academic Texts

artifacts.claimText: University of Sharjah's AI academic-texts policy applies to master’s and doctoral theses, thesis proposals and research plans, thesis-derived scientific publications, graduate-level courses and programs, and academic texts submitted to Graduate Studies bodies.

citation.citationTitle: Policy on the Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence in Academic Texts
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy on the Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence in Academic Texts
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The provisions of this policy apply to: 1. Master’s and doctoral theses. 2. Thesis proposals and research plans. 3. Scientific publications stemming from scientific theses 4. Graduate-level courses and programs, including related assignments, coursework, and projects. 5. All academic texts submitted to the Graduate Studies Council, its committees, or thesis supervision and examination committees.

artifacts.claimText: For graduate theses, University of Sharjah permits AI use only within specified support limits such as linguistic and editorial support, organizational support, and non-generative conceptual support.

citation.citationTitle: Policy on the Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence in Academic Texts
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy on the Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence in Academic Texts
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The use of artificial intelligence is permitted strictly within the following limits: Grammar and language correction; Improvement of academic style; Rephrasing without altering the scholarly meaning; Suggesting general chapter structures; Organizing ideas authored by the student; Non-Generative Conceptual Support.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Article (5): Permitted Uses, First: Graduate Theses

artifacts.claimText: For the covered graduate context, University of Sharjah strictly forbids AI-based tools in exams and quizzes and treats extensive use of ChatGPT or other generative AI tools to complete assignments as academically dishonest.

citation.citationTitle: Policy on the Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence in Academic Texts
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy on the Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence in Academic Texts
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The use of AI based tools is strictly forbidden in exams and quizzes. Extensive utilization of AI language models such as ChatGPT or any other Generative AI tools to complete your assignment is considered academically dishonest, as it is equal to having someone write your paper, take your quiz, or finish your assignment.

artifacts.claimText: University of Sharjah requires disclosure of AI use for coursework, thesis proposals, and final thesis submissions through a Declaration of Artificial Intelligence Use form.

citation.citationTitle: Policy on the Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence in Academic Texts

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy on the Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence in Academic Texts
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Disclosure of artificial intelligence use is mandatory for course works, thesis proposals and final thesis submissions. Disclosure shall be made through an official form entitled: “Declaration of Artificial Intelligence Use.” The disclosure must specify the tools used, the nature of use, and the scope and limitations of such use.

artifacts.claimText: University of Sharjah states that plagiarism and AI-text detection tools may be used only as supportive instruments and that no disciplinary decision shall be based solely on automated detection output.

citation.citationTitle: Policy on the Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence in Academic Texts

artifacts.sourceTitle: Policy on the Responsible Use of Artificial Intelligence in Academic Texts
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The University may employ multiple, complementary mechanisms, including: Human academic review and expert judgment. Plagiarism and AI-text detection tools, used solely as supportive instruments. Oral examination and requests for detailed scholarly clarification. No disciplinary decision shall be based solely on the output of automated detection tools.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Article (10): Detection Tools and Review Mechanisms

artifacts.notes: Claim wording is scoped to the policy's stated application and does not present the staged candidate as canonical.
Claim uses 'permits' and 'specified support limits' to match the source's limited permitted-use language.
Claim is limited to the covered graduate context and uses the source's strict forbidden and academically dishonest language.
Source says disclosure is mandatory for coursework, thesis proposals, and final thesis submissions.
Claim is scoped to the policy context and uses prohibits rather than a broader institution-wide data-classification claim.
Claim keeps the source's supportive-instrument and no-sole-automated-decision limits.

artifacts.title: University of Sharjah AI Academic Texts Policy Candidate
artifacts.summary: University of Sharjah has an official Graduate Studies PDF policy regulating responsible AI use in academic texts. The staged claims are limited to the policy's graduate theses, graduate-level courses, disclosure, prohibited uses, confidential-data, and detection-review language. Annual-report and event-style official results were not used as policy evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are staged agent-reviewed candidates only and are not marked human-reviewed or canonical.

The identified official policy is scoped to Graduate Studies academic texts, theses, proposals, and graduate-level coursework; it should not be broadened to all undergraduate or university-wide contexts without additional official evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
